bugs.description: create_shared_session_maker() checks `if database_url not in _session_makers` without holding `_engine_lock`. Two concurrent callers can both find the key missing, both create a session maker, and the second overwrites the first in the dict. Any sessions already created from the first session maker are now orphaned ΓÇö their parent session maker is no longer tracked, so those connections leak out of the pool accounting. The engine creation directly below IS protected by _engine_lock, but session maker creation is not.

allow_request() reads _half_open_calls and _state without holding _lock, and the caller must separately call increment_half_open_calls() in a distinct async step. This TOCTOU race means N concurrent coroutines can all call allow_request() before any of them calls increment_half_open_calls(), and all see _half_open_calls == 0 < half_open_max_calls (default 1), causing all N to return True. This floods the recovering upstream with concurrent probe requests instead of the intended single trial, defeating HALF_OPEN limiting entirely.

get_all_context() breaks out of the loop entirely on the first context whose size would exceed the budget (when contexts is non-empty). Contexts are sorted by relevance score descending, so a large but highly-relevant context will cause all subsequent smaller contexts ΓÇö which could fit within the remaining budget ΓÇö to be silently skipped. The LLM receives incomplete context, causing incorrect automation suggestions and missed domain knowledge for all 50+ downstream services using this registry.
